findings.title: Optimize 9.6MB headshot image
findings.what: Resize 5477x3651 JPEG to ~1200px wide and compress to WebP. Add Next.js Image optimization config in next.config.ts (remotePatterns/formats).
findings.why: 9.6MB image tanks First Contentful Paint and Largest Contentful Paint. A properly sized WebP would be ~50-100KB — 100x smaller.

findings.title: Delete dead component files
findings.what: Remove 6 unused component files (Hero, About, Skills, Contact, Footer, Sidebar) and 2 empty theme stubs. Portfolio.tsx implements all sections inline — these are leftover from the pre-v2 design.
findings.why: Dead code confuses contributors and bloats the repo. Sidebar.tsx and both theme files are 0 bytes.

findings.title: Configure Next.js image optimization
findings.what: Add image config: deviceSizes, formats (webp/avif), and unoptimized:false. Currently next.config.ts is completely empty — no image optimization is configured.
findings.why: Next.js Image component can auto-serve responsive, compressed images but only when configured. Without it, all images in /public are served raw.

findings.title: Add metadata and SEO essentials
findings.what: Add robots.txt, sitemap.xml, structured data (JSON-LD Person schema), and canonical URL. Layout has basic OG tags but missing these fundamentals.
findings.why: Portfolio site's primary job is discoverability. Missing robots.txt and sitemap means search engines may not index project detail pages properly.

findings.title: Prune unused shadcn/ui components
findings.what: 67 shadcn/ui components are installed but Portfolio.tsx uses only a handful (button, badge, card). Audit imports and remove unused ones (accordion, calendar, carousel, chart, command, context-menu, drawer, menubar, pagination, radio-group, resizable, table, etc.).
findings.why: ~60 unused UI component files add noise to the repo and slow IDE indexing. Removing them cuts component count by ~80%.

findings.title: Add basic error and loading states
findings.what: Create loading.tsx (skeleton UI) and not-found.tsx pages. Currently hitting an invalid project slug or slow load shows default Next.js error pages.
findings.why: Custom error pages maintain brand consistency and prevent visitors from seeing raw Next.js 404s, which looks unprofessional for a portfolio.

findings.title: Extract hardcoded data from Portfolio.tsx
findings.what: Portfolio.tsx has experience entries and skills data hardcoded inline (~515 lines). Extract to src/data/experience.ts and src/data/skills.ts, matching the pattern already used by src/data/projects.ts.
findings.why: Separating data from presentation makes content updates trivial and keeps the main component focused on layout/interaction logic. Projects already follow this pattern.
